original_abstract:
In response to the growing global focus on high-impact higher education policies, Romania underwent a period of intensive reform between 2009 and 2013 aimed at modernizing its system within the framework of the Bologna Process. This volume draws on research from the "Higher Education Evidence-Based Policy Making" project, implemented by UEFISCDI to bolster the capacity of public administration through rigorous data and international best practices. By examining critical policy domains—including quality assurance, internationalization, equity, and student-centered learning—this research evaluates the impact of the National Education Law (Law 1/2011) and its subsequent implementation. 

The analysis traces the evolution of the Romanian system from its post-communist expansion and institutional diversification to current efforts at creating a competitive European and global presence. Despite significant legislative strides and Romania’s active leadership in the European Higher Education Area (EHEA), the findings highlight persistent challenges such as demographic decline, funding imbalances, and the need for deeper institutional ownership of reforms. Ultimately, this volume provides well-documented analysis and concrete, evidence-based policy proposals intended to inform future solutions for a more equitable, high-quality, and internationally integrated higher education system in Romania.
humanized_abstract:
Romania was recently engaged in a sustained policy reform effort to address increasingly internationally spotlighted priority higher education policy issues, such as modernising the whole higher education system in line with the Bologna Process. This volume is based on the results of the activity implemented by UEFISCDI through the project “Higher Education Evidence-Based Policy Making”, focused on strengthening the capacity of public institutions to inform policy decisions with high quality research and good practices exchanged internationally. The research focuses on several key policy areas, such as quality assurance, internationalisation, equal opportunities, and student-centred learning, in order to assess the impact of the National Education Law 1/2011 and its implementation at different levels.

This book follows the development of the Romanian higher education system from the period of rapid expansion and diversification in the immediate post-communist years to the present times, during which Romania aspires to become a significant player on the European and world markets. Based on a mixture of qualitative and quantitative research, and analysing a broad spectrum of factors that have an impact on current developments and reform processes, the study points to some of the contentious issues and obstacles that must be overcome, including a declining population, resource disparities and inadequate take-up by institutions of the reforms currently under way. As a result, this volume presents, based on thorough analyses and sound empirical research, a variety of options and policy recommendations that are designed to contribute to the stabilisation of higher education in Romania in the future and to bring about fairer distribution, better quality and a higher degree of integration with the international higher education system.
